title: Negative Sampling
concept: To avoid updating millions of weights per step, negative sampling updates only the correct context word and a few random incorrect words, drastically cutting compute.

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea behind saving computer energy-saving trick behind Negative Sampling?

options.text: Updating only a few words instead of the whole dictionary
Deleting bad words from our datasets entirely
Making the AI guess only negative numbers

exercises.explain: To avoid calculating updates for millions of words at every single step, we use negative sampling to focus only on a tiny, manageable subset.

cards.title: A Math Nightmare
cards.body: Imagine trying to learn a language, and every time you get a single word right, your teacher makes you review all 100,000 wrong words you *didn't* say. Sounds exhausting, right? That's what AI models face without smart shortcuts!

cards.title: What is Negative Sampling?
cards.body: Negative Sampling is a training shortcut. Instead of updating the model's math for every single wrong word in the dictionary at every step, we only update the correct word and a tiny handful of random incorrect ('negative') words.

cards.title: The Lazy Spelling Bee
cards.body: Imagine a spelling bee with a dictionary of 100,000 words. Instead of checking if you got all 99,999 other words wrong every time you spell 'apple' correctly, the judge only checks 'apple' and randomly picks 5 other words to make sure you didn't just guess.

cards.title: Sampling in Action
cards.body: In the sentence 'The cat sat on the mat', if the target word is 'cat', 'mat' is a positive sample. Instead of calculating math for 'pizza', 'spaceship', and 500,000 other words, we randomly pick just 'pizza' and 'banana' as negative samples and ignore the rest!

cards.title: Do We Need All Words?
cards.body: People often think we must train the AI on every word in the dictionary at every single step to make it smart. In reality, focusing on the correct word and just a few random incorrect ones gets us the exact same quality of AI, but thousands of times faster!

cards.title: Slow vs. Fast Training
cards.body: Compare how the AI updates its memory bank during training. Notice how much lighter the workload is with negative sampling!

left.text: Updates the target word AND all 1,000,000 wrong words in the dictionary. Extremely slow.

right.text: Updates the target word AND only 5 random wrong words. Super fast!

exercises.prompt: Why do we need negative sampling instead of updating every word during training?

options.text: Updating millions of weights for every single word takes too much time and compute
Incorrect words are automatically deleted from the dictionary forever
The AI gets confused if it sees too many correct words at the same time
It makes the AI completely forget how to spell common words

exercises.explain: Updating millions of words for every single training step is extremely slow and takes massive amounts of computer power. Negative sampling solves this.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Negative sampling updates every single incorrect word in the vocabulary during each training step.
exercises.explain: Negative sampling only updates a few randomly selected incorrect words per step, not all of them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how we optimize training:
exercises.sentence: Instead of updating all vocabulary weights, we use ________ sampling to only update a few incorrect words.
exercises.explain: Negative sampling updates the correct word and a small, random selection of incorrect (negative) words to speed up training.

exercises.prompt: Identify which system is actively using random negative samples (noise words) to train itself against a correct target:

options.text: Prompt: 'The dog barked at the...' -> AI: 'mailman'
Prompt: 'I love hot...' -> AI: 'cocoa' (Negative samples picked: 'calculator', 'frog', 'jupiter')

exercises.explain: The prompt 'I love hot cocoa' has a realistic positive context. Option C is pure random noise generated by an AI using negative sampling selection.

exercises.prompt: You are optimizing a massive word AI. Instead of calculating updates for 1,000,000 words on every step, how does Negative Sampling solve this computational bottleneck?

options.text: It updates the weights for every single word in our vocabulary except the correct one
It updates the correct word and a tiny random sample of incorrect words, ignoring the rest
It deletes all incorrect words from the model's vocabulary to save hard drive space
It randomly swaps correct words with incorrect ones to test if the model is paying attention

exercises.explain: Negative sampling keeps the target word (positive sample) and pairs it with just a handful of random noise words (negative samples), ignoring the remaining millions of words in the vocabulary for that step!
